**TRIAL NAME:** Study on When Gallbladder Surgery is Needed for Gallstones

**MATCH SCORE:** 10.0/10

**WHY YOU MAY QUALIFY:**
- You have pain in your upper right belly. The pain lasts more than 30 minutes.
- A scan showed you have gallstones in your gallbladder.
- You have bad belly pain. The pain has lasted more than 8-12 hours.
- Your doctor found it hurts when they press on your upper right belly.
- Tests show your gallbladder is swollen. You may have had a fever.

**THINGS TO CLARIFY WITH YOUR DOCTOR:**
- You seem to meet all the study needs. No other information is needed.

**LEARN MORE:**
https://clinicaltrials.gov/study/NCT01190280

**TRIAL NAME:** Study of Adults with Bad Stomach Pain in Emergency Rooms

**MATCH SCORE:** 10.0/10

**WHY YOU MAY QUALIFY:**
- You are an adult with stomach pain

**THINGS TO TALK ABOUT WITH YOUR DOCTOR:**
- (No other information needed - you seem to meet the main requirement)

**LEARN MORE:**
https://clinicaltrials.gov/study/NCT03074084

**MATCH SCORE:** 6.7/10

**MATCH SCORE:** 5.0/10

**THINGS TO ASK YOUR DOCTOR:**

**LEARN MORE:**
https://clinicaltrials.gov/study/NCT02852408

**TRIAL NAME:** Heart Changes in People with Gallbladder Problems

**MATCH SCORE:** 3.3/10

*Note: This low score means this trial may not be right for you.*

**WHY YOU MAY QUALIFY:**
- You are between 18 and 75 years old

**THINGS TO ASK YOUR DOCTOR:**
- Do I have serious gallbladder problems? (Like acute cholecystitis, biliary colic, or acute cholangitis? These are problems that make people go to the emergency room)
- Can I join this study? Am I willing to sign the consent form?

**LEARN MORE:**
https://clinicaltrials.gov/study/NCT01706068

**TRIAL NAME:** Study of Blood Protein Levels in Hepatitis C Patients

**MATCH SCORE:** 10.0/10

**WHY YOU MAY QUALIFY:**
- You are between 18 and 70 years old
- You have hepatitis C virus in your blood
- Your weight and height don't matter for this study
- It doesn't matter if you had hepatitis C treatment before
- Any level of liver scarring is okay

**THINGS TO ASK YOUR DOCTOR:**
- Is my bilirubin level above 2 mg/dl? (This shows how well my liver cleans waste)
- Is my albumin level below 2.8 g/dl? (This shows a protein my liver makes)
- Is my INR 1.7 or higher? (This shows how fast my blood clots)
- Is my platelet count below 50,000? (Platelets help your blood clot)
- Is my creatinine above 2.5 mg/dl? (This shows how well my kidneys work)

**LEARN MORE:**
https://clinicaltrials.gov/study/NCT03882307

**MATCH SCORE:** 2.5/10

**WHY YOU MAY QUALIFY:**

**THINGS TO CLARIFY WITH YOUR DOCTOR:**

- Do I need help breathing from a machine (ventilator) right now?
- Does my age fit what this study is looking for compared to other patients?
- Does my lung function test results match what this study needs?

**LEARN MORE:**
https://clinicaltrials.gov/study/NCT03680495

*Note: This trial may not be the best fit for you based on your low match score. Your doctor can help you explore other options that might be more suitable.*

**TRIAL NAME:** Study of Early Cervical Cancer Signs and Causes

**MATCH SCORE:** 10.0/10

**WHY YOU MAY QUALIFY:**
- You are a woman who needs a colposcopy (a close look at your cervix)
- You have early stage cervical cancer
- You are 18 years old or older

**THINGS TO ASK YOUR DOCTOR:**
- Is your referral to the University of Oklahoma?
- Do you have HIV/AIDS? This study needs to know.

**LEARN MORE:**
https://clinicaltrials.gov/study/NCT00339989

**TRIAL NAME:** Study of Life Quality After Different Cervical Cancer Surgeries

**MATCH SCORE:** 6.7/10

**WHY YOU MAY QUALIFY:**
- You have cervical cancer that has spread to nearby tissue
- Your cancer is in the early stages that this study looks at
- You are between 18 and 45 years old

**THINGS TO ASK YOUR DOCTOR:**
- Am I healthy enough for surgery? Have I signed the consent forms?
- Have I agreed to have my cervix removed or all my reproductive organs removed?
- Can I read and speak English well enough to fill out surveys?
- Do I understand this study? Can I give my consent?

**LEARN MORE:**
https://clinicaltrials.gov/study/NCT00579787

**TRIAL NAME:** Comparing Two Types of Hysterectomy for Early Cervical Cancer

**MATCH SCORE:** 6.0/10

**WHY YOU MAY QUALIFY:**
- You are 18 years old or older
- You do not want to have children in the future

**THINGS TO ASK YOUR DOCTOR:**
- Do I have cervical cancer that is confirmed? Is it one of these types: adenocarcinoma, squamous, or adenosquamous?
- Is my cervical cancer low-risk and early-stage? Is it FIGO Stage IA2 or IB1?
- Have I had all the physical exams I need? This includes a recto-vaginal exam and cervix check.
- Have I had the imaging tests I need? This includes chest x-ray or CT scan and pelvic MRI.
- Am I healthy enough for surgery?
- Am I willing to fill out forms about my quality of life for the study?
- Am I willing to take part in the cost study portion?
- Can I give proper consent to join this research?
- Will I be able to come to all treatment visits and follow-up appointments?
- Can my surgery happen within 20 weeks of when I was first diagnosed?

**LEARN MORE:**
https://clinicaltrials.gov/study/NCT01658930

**TRIAL NAME:** Testing Erythropoietin Medicine for Hemolytic Uremic Syndrome

**MATCH SCORE:** 10.0/10

**WHY YOU MAY QUALIFY:**

- Your illness began with stomach problems. Then you got blood and kidney problems.
- Your red blood cells are damaged. Your platelet count is low. Your kidneys have problems.
- Blood tests show high creatinine levels. You have protein or blood in your urine.

**THINGS TO CLARIFY WITH YOUR DOCTOR:**
- (No additional information needed from your medical records)

**TRIAL NAME:** Testing Eculizumab Medicine for Children with Kidney Disease from E. Coli

**MATCH SCORE:** 8.0/10

**WHY YOU MAY QUALIFY:**
- You are a child between 1 month and 18 years old
- Your platelet count is low (less than 150,000)
- You have anemia. This means your red blood cells are damaged and your hemoglobin is low.
- You had diarrhea before getting sick. Or you tested positive for bad E. coli bacteria.

**THINGS TO ASK YOUR DOCTOR:**
- Do I have kidney problems for this study?
- Can my parents give written permission for me to join?

**LEARN MORE:**
https://clinicaltrials.gov/study/NCT02205541

**WHY YOU MAY QUALIFY:**

- You have low red blood cells. The blood cells look damaged under a microscope.

**THINGS TO ASK YOUR DOCTOR:**
